they definitely lost all those impulse buys. the price was what motivated me to buy one. i didn't have much at the time, so i bought a 256gb lcd model, which i had upgraded to a1tb drive down the line. i ended up buying a 1tb oled model because i loved it so much. now with (i hate to say it) nintendo switch 2 being $500. it has become the handheld that makes the most sense.

so: steam deck out of stock, people mad at valve because out of stock steam deck back in stock with higher prices because they cost more to make, people mad at valve because price is higher. steam deck sells out in less than 24 hours. people mad at valve because not enough stock. was also the same story with the controller - "$99 is way too much!" - sold out in 25 minutes. nobody likes to pay more for things, even when that cost is justified by higher costs to make (or quality) - but they damn well pay it because "expensive" is relative, while "nonexistent" is very not. the steam machine failed before because (1) third parties doing what was best for them and not valve (or the end user), and (2) it expected devs to port to linux, which was overly optimistic. the new steam machine is a reference model designed and made by valve directly, and *plays all of your old games unmodified* because of tech like proton that didn't exist the first time around. say what you will about valve, they aren't stupid. they make mistakes, but they learn from them.

i decided to upgrade from the 15 to the 17 to take advantage of the $300 trade-in on the 15, and using id me shop (veterans, 1st responders, teachers and others are eligible with verification) 10% off at apple, i was able to get the 17 for $446. that price, those features, and the fact that i usually wait about 3 years to upgrade my (any carrier phone) - coming up on that now - made the decision an easy one. as a bonus, i had just recently signed up for a credit card that gave me a $200 credit after i spent a certain amount within the first few months, and that credit just dropped into my account! my new 17 will cost me $246, and i'm so excited to have so much more storage, camera improvements, faster chip, excellent referesh rate, ai, and a beautiful, bright screen with a two hour increase in battery life on the phone. seems like this model hits the sweet spot for most consumers.

i can't recommend getting any new video game hardware right now. prices going up made things too volatile. i'd say stick with what you have right now, console or pc. all i can recommend are retro emulation handhelds like the stuff from anbernic as well as android handhelds like the ayn odin 3, but even those will likely see price increases. maybe the xbox rog ally (standard version, not x). as expensive as that is, it's way cheaper and way more powerful than the steam deck, although not as powerful as the ally x. it has a much better price-to-performance ratio than the steam deck now. i would also consider checking out the used market if you're looking for pc parts, but watch out for scalpers. when buying a gaming pc or a console, you have to take everything into consideration. "what can i do with my machine?" "how's the price-to-performance ratio?" "do the amount of games make this machine worth buying?" "will it last me a long time?" things are very bad right now.
